name: Spanish Tortilla
tagline: Slow-cooked potato and onion omelette — bar-stand classic

hero.alt: Sliced Spanish tortilla showing layered potato and egg
hero.caption: tortilla española — three ingredients

steps.title: Slice paper-thin
steps.detail: Slice potato 1/8-inch thick. Slice onion thin. Salt both — it'll start releasing water.
steps.tip: A mandoline gives even slices — even cooking.

steps.title: Slow-poach in olive oil
steps.detail: Heat olive oil in a 10-inch non-stick pan over medium-low. Slip in potatoes and onion. Cook 25 min, turning occasionally — they should soften but not crisp.
steps.tip: It's not frying, it's confiting. Bubbles should be gentle.

steps.title: Mix with eggs
steps.detail: Whisk eggs with salt. Drain potato-onion mix (save oil), fold into eggs. Rest 10 min — flavors meld.
steps.tip: Resting lets eggs absorb potato starch — that's the silky texture.

steps.title: Cook and flip
steps.detail: Heat 2 tbsp of the reserved oil in the pan. Pour in mixture. Cook 5 min until edges set. Cover with a plate, invert, slide back in. Cook 3 min more.
steps.tip: Flip is dramatic but works. Confidence beats hesitation.

healthFacts: Egg + potato together provide complete protein and slow carbs.
Olive oil cooked low retains polyphenols — frying poaches the potato.
